title: Name changes
wikitext:
[]: Name changes             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:13 pm So, the title was changed from New Century Evangelion to Neon Genesis Evangelion. Apostles were changed to Angels, the episode titles were changed, and the Rebuild movies' names were changed. I keep hearing that this was Anno's work, and i know it is, but some idiot is insulting people over this, so can someone give me a link to prove that Anno did bring effect changes?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:15 pm A little context for this rant would be greatly appreciated.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:20 pm [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LKy7PnjsDX8&lc=MK7U_cJzr3UiAKR5NY1q6oLNK2D4umdx-GIN1xLtgWg[/url] I wish there was a way to change my sarcastic username that everyone takes seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:29 pm Donut, the guy's just a dumb troll and giving him a source isn't going to make anything better. I really hope this doesn't come across as hard, i just wouldn't want you to face any more pain on the matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:29 pm It looks like your tormenter may actually be right here. Wikipedia wrote: As was done with episode titles in the original series, each film has an original Japanese title and a separate English international title picked out by the Japanese studio itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:43 pm Here you go, buddy. The Evageeks Wikipedia wrote: The actual Japanese title is Shin Seiki Evangelion, a hybrid Japanese-Greek title which means "Gospel of the New Century". The title Neon Genesis Evangelion is all Greek, and means "Gospel of the New Genesis". However, this is not a case of the English-language distributors messing with the translation. Neon Genesis Evangelion is the official English title, chosen by the series' Japanese creators.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:44 pm OH THANK GOD. If I had to concede that the YouTube troller was correct I would have punched somebody.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:47 pm There is an article that gwern posted some time ago that had Anno say that specifically, but that would take time. Time as in, going into each individual thread, going into the article, and CTRL-F type, "I thought it was cool."

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:53 pm Stryker wrote: There is an article that gwern posted some time ago that had Anno say that specifically, but that would take time. Time as in, going into each individual thread, going into the article, and CTRL-F type, "I thought it was cool."

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Mar 14, 2013 7:59 pm Didn't someone post an image of Anno with the "I thought it was cool." caption recently? There may have been a link to the interview somewhere in the vicinity.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 15, 2013 2:50 am Stryker wrote: CTRL-F type, "I thought it was cool."

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri Mar 15, 2013 3:14 am Doing that on EGF (not sure if i did it correctly) doesn't seem to bring any results pertinent to Anno, either.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Mar 15, 2013 3:31 am Anno said this about "Evangelion" during that interview with children: which is probably what riffraff was recalling, but Anno is notoriously the master troll of Japanese animation, so he also provided this pretentious explanation for the "Neon Genesis/Shin Seiki" part in the 11th disc of the Renewal (the extra one unreleased in the western world): Anno being 2deep4u wrote: ''New Era'' becomes ''Neon Genesis'' in English, the ''Era'' is translated using its alternate meaning; on the other hand, its Japanese counterpart has another meaning, this is done to express both meanings. The word doesn't look very good in Katakana, so we used English and Japanese to play on the pun. This comes from our desire to make something new from the anime, that's all. It means both the new era and the new genesis that mark that, that's what the title means.

Article: Name changes
 is probably what riffraff was recalling, but Anno is notoriously the master troll of Japanese animation, so he also provided this pretentious explanation for the "Neon Genesis/Shin Seiki" part in the 11th disc of the Renewal (the extra one unreleased in the western world): Anno being 2deep4u wrote: ''New Era'' becomes ''Neon Genesis'' in English, the ''Era'' is translated using its alternate meaning; on the other hand, its Japanese counterpart has another meaning, this is done to express both meanings. The word doesn't look very good in Katakana, so we used English and Japanese to play on the pun. This comes from our desire to make something new from the anime, that's all. It means both the new era and the new genesis that mark that, that's what the title means.
